executiveSummary: Franklin County commissioned a new 1,560-square-foot Coroner's Office morgue at 1310 N 5th Avenue in Pasco to establish local, purpose-built autopsy and related death-investigation functions. MH Construction served as general contractor and delivered the facility from a multidisciplinary design package.
clientBackground: The Franklin County Coroner's Office investigates suspicious, unnatural, violent, and sudden unexplained deaths within Franklin County and serves affected families through local public-sector operations.
challenge: Deliver a secure, purpose-built municipal facility for receiving, refrigeration, examination, and observation functions while coordinating multidisciplinary design requirements at an active county property.
scopeOfWork: General contracting for construction of the 1,560-square-foot, one-story Coroner's Office morgue
Coordination of civil, architectural, structural, mechanical, plumbing, and electrical work
Construction of secure receiving and release garage access
Installation and coordination of walk-in refrigeration, examination, and observation areas
Delivery of restroom, interior components, building systems, and associated exterior work

timelineAndMilestones.name: Professional-services RFQ issued
timelineAndMilestones.note: Franklin County opened procurement for planning, design, and construction-phase management services.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Design set issued
timelineAndMilestones.note: Meier Architecture | Engineering issued public Coroner's Morgue Addition plans.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Facility completion reported
timelineAndMilestones.note: Independent local reporting identified completion on September 1, 2025.

timelineAndMilestones.name: Community grand opening
timelineAndMilestones.note: Franklin County Coroner's Office held public opening for the new facility.

executionDetails: MH Construction coordinated municipal construction against a multidisciplinary design package and referenced code requirements, integrating secure vehicle access, refrigeration, examination, observation, and supporting building systems in a compact one-story footprint.
outcomesAndImpact: Established local on-site autopsy capability for Franklin County
Reduced reliance on out-of-county travel for procedures
Improved operational coordination with law enforcement, funeral services, and donor-recovery partners
Delivered secure receiving, refrigeration, examination, and observation functions at county level

clientTestimonial.quote: I'm so glad the way it turned out and the way it works for us. It's a really nice flow.
clientTestimonial.name: Curtis McGary
clientTestimonial.title: Franklin County Coroner

safetyAndComplianceNotes.projectSpecificSafetyProtocols: Contract documents assign site-condition verification and jobsite safety responsibility to the contractor.
safetyAndComplianceNotes.codesReferenced: 2021 building, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, energy, accessibility, and life-safety requirements

approvalAndPublishingNotes.redactionsNeeded: Confirm media credit and usage rights, quote reuse approval, owner approver, and final inspection status before publication.
